acceptedQuestions.subject: Environment
acceptedQuestions.prompt:
With reference to the Global Goal on Adaptation (GGA) and related frameworks, consider the following statements:
1. The GGA was formally established under the Glasgow Climate Pact.
2. The measurement framework for tracking adaptation progress under the GGA formalizes a four-stage cycle.
3. The Baku Adaptation Roadmap establishes adaptation work for the period 2026-2028, leading up to the next Global Stocktake.

How many of the above statements are correct?
acceptedQuestions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Global Goal on Adaptation (GGA) was established under Article 7.1 of the Paris Agreement (2015), not the Glasgow Climate Pact. Statement 2 is correct: The GGA measurement framework formalizes a four-stage cycle and adopts indicators across 7 thematic sectors. Statement 3 is correct: The Baku Adaptation Roadmap establishes adaptation work for 2026-2028, leading up to the next Global Stocktake of the Paris Agreement.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: The GGA was established under the Paris Agreement (2015). Its measurement framework utilizes a four-stage cycle, while the Baku Adaptation Roadmap guides adaptation work until the next Global Stocktake.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Measurement framework for tracking adaptation progress under the Global Goal on Adaptation (GGA): Indicators adopted across 7 thematic sectors. Formalizes a four-stage cycle. Global Goal on Adaptation (GGA) was established under Article 7.1 of the Paris Agreement (2015). Baku Adaptation Roadmap: Approves and establishes the work for 2026-2028, until the next Global Stocktake of Paris Agreement.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: Strong question utilizing the 'How many statements are correct' format. Tests the origin of GGA and the timeline of the Baku Adaptation Roadmap effectively.

reviewerNotes: Selected 3 questions that comprehensively cover the provided text without overlapping. Candidate 3 was rejected due to redundancy with Candidates 1 and 2. Candidate 5 was rejected as the Assertion-Reason format felt forced for a simple factual relationship. Rewrote the prompts and options to align with UPSC standards.
